title: (No AI) How one founder’s bet on ‘the old school web’ is paying off
author: Allison Johnson
content:
![Screenshot showing a historical map of Seattle overlaid on a current map](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Screenshot-2026-05-27-at-11.17.27AM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A good time with old maps. | Image: Past Maps

Craig Campbell walked away from the river of investor money flowing into AI to create, of all things, a website.

Sure, Campbell probably could have started an AI company. He's a former engineer at Meta and an experienced tech founder who in 2022 sold his last venture - an e-commerce tool for businesses that use Shopify - right as the AI boom was booming. "I had my prior VC investors breathing down my neck, going 'start something else. We'll write you a blank check.'" He had other ideas.

People generally aren't rushing to get into the website business, what with [the Google Zero event horizon](https://www.theverge.com/24167865/google-zero-search-crash-housefresh-ai-overviews-traffic-data-audience) approaching. Campbell was undeterred and has gro …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/938245/past-maps-website-google-zero-ai)

A good time with old maps. | Image: Past Maps

Sure, Campbell probably could have started an AI company. He's a former engineer at Meta and an experienced tech founder who in 2022 sold his last venture - an e-commerce tool for businesses that use Shopify  - right as the AI boom was booming. "I had my prior VC investors breathing down my neck, going 'start something else. We'll write you a blank check.'" He had other ideas.
People generally aren't rushing to get into the website business, what with the Google Zero event horizon approaching. Campbell was undeterred and has gro …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) AI grifters are creating fake Black people to sell Shein junk
author: Nicole Froio
content:
![three screenshots of Black TikTok sellers, apparently AI generated, with a green background and belt buckle inlays](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/268550_Crying_AI_avatars_AParkin_2.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

TikTok sellers that appear to be AI generated, in tears. | The Verge

Aliyah, a light-skinned Black woman dressed in country-western gear, is struggling to sell metal buckles she handmade on TikTok. In a [video](https://www.tiktok.com/@aliyahsbuckles.store/video/7614301114329992478?q=belt%20buckle&t=1777935944306) for the social media platform from March, she cries to the camera and pleads for views: "Even as a black woman, I have more faith that white women will stay 13 seconds \[on this video\] to save my belt buckle business," the onscreen text reads. She wipes a tear off her cheek.

But Aliyah isn't real, and neither are her supposedly [handmade products](https://aliyahsbuckles.store/products/belt-blades) - she's one of many AI-generated influencers created to sell mass-produced products via dropshipping on TikTok, Facebook, and Instagram. Identical belt buckles …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/938844/ai-tiktok-shop-blackface-shein-dropshipping)
